rulings.Case Title: ANTHONY CROSETTI et al VS. MARRIOTT HOTEL SERVICES, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 6, 2012, Line 1. DEFENDANT MARRIOTT INTERNATIONAL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Denied. Mr. Crosetti's declaration creates a triable issue whether he stayed at defendant's hotel. Assuming that California law applies to the issues of negligence and neither side has argued otherwise, defendant's duty is to use reasonable care to keep its property in a reasonably safe condition. (CACO 1001). Whether defendant breached that duty by not providing a bath mat is a question of fact to be decided by the trier of fact. Defendant's objections 4-6 are overruled. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: SABEER BHATIA, ET. AL. VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Interrogatory Responses And Awarding Monetary Sanctions And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 6, 2012, line 1, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, A, EAST-WEST INVESTMENT, INC.,A CALIFORNIA Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order Compelling Interrogatory Responses And Awarding Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition to Defendant East West Bank's motion for an order compelling interrogatory responses and awarding monetary sanctions having been filed, the motion is GRANTED. Plaintiff USI shall serve verified responses without objection to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set Two, and Plaintiff Bhatia shall respond without objection and produce documents in response to Defendant's Special Interrogatories, Set Two, Special Interrogatories, Set Three and Requests for Production, Set Three. Defendant's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $425.The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: SABEER BHATIA, ET. AL. VS. EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order 1) Compelling Deposition Of Pltf With Document Production 2) Awarding Monetary Sanctions And 3) Setting Hearing On Summary Judgment Mtns Within 30 Days Of Trial And Req. For Judicial Ntc
rulings.Rulings: On calendar for Friday, January 6, 2012, line 1, DEFENDANT EAST WEST BANK, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION, A, EAST-WEST INVESTMENT, INC.,A CALIFORNIA Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For An Order 1) Compelling Deposition Of Pltf With Document Production 2) Awarding Monetary Sanctions And 3) Setting Hearing On Summary Judgment Mtns Within 30 Days Of Trial And Req. For Judicial Ntc Pro Tem Judge Ernst Halperin, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant East West Bank's motion to compel the deposition of Plaintiff / Cross-Defendant Sabeer Bhathia with document production is GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff did not serve objections to Defendant's deposition notice. Plaintiff shall appear for deposition on January 11th and produce the documents requested in the deposition notice. Good cause exists under CCP 437c(a) to allow a motion for summary judgment to be heard less than 30 days before the trial date, and the deadline for a hearing on a motion for summary judgment is set for April 11,2012. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JEREMY MANN et al VS. SEAN MULVANEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 6, 2012, Line 9. DEFENDANT SEAN MULVANEY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied. The motion is moot because it is directed at the complaint, which is no longer the operative pleading. (State Compensation Insurance Fund v. Superior Court (2010) 184 Cal. App. 4th 1124, 1131). Moreover, defendant failed to satisfy his burden of production of presenting sufficient facts to show that plaintiffs will be unable to prevail on their request for punitive damages. Further, even if defendant had satisfied its burden, plaintiffs presented sufficient evidence to create a triable issue regarding their entitlement to punitive damages. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VISA INC. VS. CERTAIN UNDERWRITERS AT LLOYD'S, LONDON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 6, 2012, Line 11. PLAINTIFF VISA INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Denied as to issue one and granted as to the remaining issues. Per the parties' insurance contract, defendant did not have an immediate duty to pay for plaintiff's defense costs because the $10 million class action deductible covers both damages and defense costs. Further, per the parties' insurance contract, the monetary amounts awarded pursuant to Penal Code 637.2(a) and the applicable provisions of the laws of the other states are neither "penalties" nor "sanctions." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. = (302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: STANLEY SHEN VS. ALICE TOY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, January 6, 2012, Line 17. DEFENDANTS ALICE TOY AND HA HUYNH'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled as to the first through fifth causes of action and sustained without leave to amend as to the sixth cause of action. The first five causes of action are adequately pled. Whether the contractual term "fixture" covers some of all of the sewer system is not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. A claim for breach of the warranty of habitability is not available unless there are inhabitable conditions, but that is not the case for a non-residential restaurant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
